name: brew
description: The missing package manager for macOS

subcommands.name: install
subcommands.description: Install a formula or cask

name: --cask
options.description: Treat all named arguments as casks

name: --formula
options.description: Treat all named arguments as formulae

name: --dry-run
-n
options.description: Show what would be installed without actually installing

name: --fetch-HEAD
options.description: Fetch the upstream repository to detect if the HEAD installation is outdated

name: --ignore-dependencies
options.description: Skip installing any dependencies of any kind

name: --only-dependencies
options.description: Install the dependencies with specified options but do not install the formula itself

name: --build-from-source
-s
options.description: Compile formula from source even if a bottle is available

name: --force-bottle
options.description: Install from a bottle if it exists for the current or newest version of macOS, even if it would not normally be used for installation

name: --include-test
options.description: Install testing dependencies required to run brew test

name: --HEAD
options.description: If formula defines it, install the HEAD version

name: --keep-tmp
options.description: Retain the temporary files created during installation

name: --overwrite
options.description: Delete files that already exist in the prefix while linking

name: --force
-f
options.description: Install formulae without checking for previously installed keg-only or non-migrated versions

name: --no-quarantine
options.description: Disable quarantining of downloads (cask)

name: --skip-cask-deps
options.description: Skip installing cask dependencies

name: --require-sha
options.description: Require all casks to have a checksum

name: --verbose
-v
options.description: Print the verification and post-install steps

name: --debug
-d
options.description: If brewing fails, open an interactive debugging session

subcommands.name: uninstall
subcommands.description: Uninstall a formula or cask

name: --cask
options.description: Treat all named arguments as casks

name: --formula
options.description: Treat all named arguments as formulae

name: --force
-f
options.description: Delete all installed versions of formula

name: --ignore-dependencies
options.description: Don't fail uninstall even if formula is a dependency of any installed formulae

name: --zap
options.description: Remove all files associated with a cask (may include files which are shared with other applications)

subcommands.name: upgrade
subcommands.description: Upgrade outdated casks and formulae

args.name: formula
args.description: Formula or cask to upgrade (upgrades all if omitted)

name: --cask
options.description: Treat all named arguments as casks

name: --formula
options.description: Treat all named arguments as formulae

name: --dry-run
-n
options.description: Show what would be upgraded without actually upgrading

name: --fetch-HEAD
options.description: Fetch the upstream repository to detect if the HEAD installation is outdated

name: --ignore-pinned
options.description: Set a successful exit status even if pinned formulae are not upgraded

name: --keep-tmp
options.description: Retain the temporary files created during installation

name: --build-from-source
-s
options.description: Compile formula from source even if a bottle is available

name: --force-bottle
options.description: Install from a bottle if it exists

name: --overwrite
options.description: Delete files that already exist in the prefix while linking

name: --force
-f
options.description: Install an older version of formula without pulling casks from a different tap

name: --greedy
options.description: Upgrade casks with auto_updates or version :latest

name: --greedy-latest
options.description: Upgrade casks with version :latest

name: --greedy-auto-updates
options.description: Upgrade casks with auto_updates

name: --no-quarantine
options.description: Disable quarantining of downloads (cask)

name: --skip-cask-deps
options.description: Skip installing cask dependencies

name: --verbose
-v
options.description: Print the verification and post-install steps

subcommands.name: update
subcommands.description: Fetch the newest version of Homebrew and all formulae

name: --merge
options.description: Use git merge to apply updates instead of git rebase

name: --auto-update
options.description: Run on auto-updates (before brew install, brew upgrade or brew tap)

name: --force
-f
options.description: Always do a slower, full update check even if unnecessary

name: --verbose
-v
options.description: Print the directories checked and git operations performed

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: List installed formulae and casks

args.name: formula
args.description: Formula or cask to list files for (optional)

name: --cask
--casks
options.description: List only casks

name: --formula
--formulae
options.description: List only formulae

name: --versions
options.description: Show the version number for installed formulae

name: --multiple
options.description: Only show formulae with multiple versions installed

name: --pinned
options.description: List only pinned formulae
Force output to be one entry per line

name: -l
options.description: List formulae and/or casks in long format

name: -r
options.description: Reverse the order of the formulae and/or casks sort

name: -t
options.description: Sort output by time modified, newest first

subcommands.name: search
subcommands.description: Perform a substring search of cask tokens and formula names

name: --cask
options.description: Search only among casks

name: --formula
options.description: Search only among formulae

name: --desc
options.description: Search for formulae with a description matching the query

name: --eval-all
options.description: Evaluate all available formulae and casks, whether installed or not

name: --repology
options.description: Search for text in the Repology database

name: --macports
options.description: Search for text in the MacPorts database

name: --fink
options.description: Search for text in the Fink database

name: --opensuse
options.description: Search for text in the OpenSUSE database

name: --fedora
options.description: Search for text in the Fedora database

name: --debian
options.description: Search for text in the Debian database

name: --ubuntu
options.description: Search for text in the Ubuntu database

subcommands.name: info
subcommands.description: Display brief statistics for your Homebrew installation or information about a formula or cask

args.name: formula
args.description: Formula or cask to show information about

name: --cask
options.description: Treat all named arguments as casks

name: --formula
options.description: Treat all named arguments as formulae

name: --analytics
options.description: List global Homebrew analytics data or, if specified, install and build error data for formula

name: --days
options.description: How many days of analytics data to retrieve (30, 90, or 365)

name: --category
options.description: Which type of analytics data to retrieve (install, install-on-request, or build-error)

name: --github
options.description: Open the GitHub source page for formula or cask in a browser

name: --json
options.description: Print a JSON representation of each formula or cask

name: --installed
options.description: Print JSON of formulae that are currently installed

name: --eval-all
options.description: Evaluate all available formulae and casks, whether installed or not

name: --variations
options.description: Include the variations hash in each formula's JSON output

name: --verbose
-v
options.description: Show more verbose analytics data for formula

subcommands.name: doctor
subcommands.description: Check your system for potential problems

name: --list-checks
options.description: List all audit methods, which can be run individually if provided as arguments

name: --audit-debug
options.description: Enable debugging and profiling of audit methods

name: --verbose
-v
options.description: Make some output more verbose

subcommands.name: cleanup
subcommands.description: Remove stale lock files and outdated downloads for all formulae and casks

args.name: formula
args.description: Formula or cask to clean up (cleans all if omitted)

name: --prune
options.description: Remove all cache files older than specified days

name: --dry-run
-n
options.description: Show what would be removed, but do not actually remove anything

name: -s
options.description: Scrub the cache, including downloads for even the latest versions

name: --prune-prefix
options.description: Only prune the symlinks and directories from the prefix and remove no other files

subcommands.name: tap
subcommands.description: Tap a formula repository

name: --full
options.description: Convert a shallow clone to a full clone without untapping

name: --shallow
options.description: Fetch tap as a shallow clone rather than a full clone

name: --force-auto-update
options.description: Auto-update tap even if it is not hosted on GitHub

name: --custom-remote
options.description: Install or change a tap with a custom remote

name: --repair
options.description: Migrate tapped formulae from symlink-based to directory-based structure

name: --list-pinned
options.description: List all pinned taps

subcommands.name: untap
subcommands.description: Remove a tapped formula repository

name: --force
-f
options.description: Untap even if formulae or casks from this tap are currently installed

subcommands.name: outdated
subcommands.description: List installed casks and formulae that have an updated version available

name: --cask
options.description: List only outdated casks

name: --formula
options.description: List only outdated formulae

name: --fetch-HEAD
options.description: Fetch the upstream repository to detect if the HEAD installation is outdated

name: --greedy
options.description: Print outdated casks with auto_updates or version :latest

name: --greedy-latest
options.description: Print outdated casks with version :latest

name: --greedy-auto-updates
options.description: Print outdated casks with auto_updates

name: --json
options.description: Print output in JSON format

name: --verbose
-v
options.description: Include detailed version information

name: --quiet
-q
options.description: List only the names of outdated kegs (takes precedence over --verbose)

subcommands.name: pin
subcommands.description: Pin the specified formulae, preventing them from being upgraded

subcommands.name: unpin
subcommands.description: Unpin formulae, allowing them to be upgraded

subcommands.name: autoremove
subcommands.description: Uninstall formulae that were only installed as a dependency of another formula and are now no longer needed

name: --dry-run
-n
options.description: Show what formulae would be uninstalled, without actually uninstalling anything

subcommands.name: deps
subcommands.description: Show dependencies for a formula or cask

args.name: formula
args.description: Formula or cask to show dependencies for

name: --cask
options.description: Treat all named arguments as casks

name: --formula
options.description: Treat all named arguments as formulae
Only show dependencies one level deep

name: -n
options.description: Sort dependencies in topological order

name: --union
options.description: Show the union of dependencies for multiple formulae, instead of the intersection

name: --full-name
options.description: List dependencies by their full name

name: --installed
options.description: List dependencies for formulae that are currently installed

name: --eval-all
options.description: Evaluate all available formulae and casks

name: --include-build
options.description: Include :build dependencies for formula

name: --include-optional
options.description: Include :optional dependencies for formula

name: --include-test
options.description: Include :test dependencies for formula

name: --skip-recommended
options.description: Skip :recommended dependencies for formula

name: --include-requirements
options.description: Include requirements in addition to dependencies for formula

name: --tree
options.description: Show dependencies as a tree

name: --graph
options.description: Show dependencies as a directed graph

name: --dot
options.description: Show text-based graph description in DOT format

name: --annotate
options.description: Mark any build, test, optional, or recommended dependencies as such

name: --direct
options.description: Show only first-order dependencies

subcommands.name: services
subcommands.description: Manage background services with macOS launchctl

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: List all managed services for the current user

subcommands.name: start
subcommands.description: Start the service formula immediately and register it to launch at login

name: --all
options.description: Start all registered services

name: --file
options.description: Use the service file from this location to start the service

subcommands.name: stop
subcommands.description: Stop the service formula immediately and unregister it from launching at login

name: --all
options.description: Stop all registered services

subcommands.name: restart
subcommands.description: Stop (if necessary) and start the service formula

name: --all
options.description: Restart all registered services

name: --file
options.description: Use the service file from this location to restart the service

subcommands.name: run
subcommands.description: Run the service formula without registering to launch at login

name: --all
options.description: Run all registered services

subcommands.name: kill
subcommands.description: Stop the service formula and unload the plist without disabling it

name: --all
options.description: Kill all registered services

subcommands.name: info
subcommands.description: Show properties of one or several services

args.name: formula
args.description: Service formula to show info for

name: --all
options.description: Show info for all registered services

name: --json
options.description: Output service info in JSON format

subcommands.name: cleanup
subcommands.description: Remove all unused services

name: --file
options.description: Use the service file from this location

name: --all
options.description: Run subcommand on all services

name: --json
options.description: Output in JSON format

subcommands.name: leaves
subcommands.description: List installed formulae that are not dependencies of another installed formula

name: --installed-on-request
-r
options.description: Only list formulae that were manually installed

name: --installed-as-dependency
-p
options.description: Only list formulae that were installed as dependencies

subcommands.name: link
subcommands.description: Symlink all of formula's installed files into the Homebrew prefix

name: --overwrite
options.description: Delete files that already exist in the prefix while linking

name: --dry-run
-n
options.description: Show what would be linked, without actually linking

name: --force
-f
options.description: Allow keg-only formulae to be linked

name: --HEAD
options.description: Link the HEAD version of the formula if it is installed

subcommands.name: unlink
subcommands.description: Remove symlinks for formula from the Homebrew prefix

name: --dry-run
-n
options.description: Show what would be unlinked, without actually unlinking

subcommands.name: uses
subcommands.description: Show formulae and casks that specify formula as a dependency

args.name: formula
args.description: Formula to find reverse dependencies for

name: --recursive
options.description: Resolve more than one level of dependencies

name: --installed
options.description: Only list formulae and casks that are currently installed

name: --eval-all
options.description: Evaluate all available formulae and casks

name: --include-build
options.description: Include all formulae that specify formula as :build type dependency

name: --include-test
options.description: Include all formulae that specify formula as :test type dependency

name: --include-optional
options.description: Include all formulae that specify formula as :optional type dependency

name: --skip-recommended
options.description: Skip all formulae that specify formula as :recommended type dependency

name: --formula
options.description: Include only formulae

name: --cask
options.description: Include only casks

subcommands.name: config
subcommands.description: Show Homebrew and system configuration info

subcommands.name: home
subcommands.description: Open a formula or cask's homepage in a browser

args.name: formula
args.description: Formula or cask whose homepage to open (opens Homebrew's own homepage if omitted)

name: --cask
options.description: Treat all named arguments as casks

name: --formula
options.description: Treat all named arguments as formulae

subcommands.name: fetch
subcommands.description: Download a bottle (if available) or source packages for formulae and binaries for casks

name: --cask
options.description: Treat all named arguments as casks

name: --formula
options.description: Treat all named arguments as formulae

name: --HEAD
options.description: Fetch HEAD version instead of stable version

name: --build-from-source
-s
options.description: Download source packages rather than a bottle

name: --build-bottle
options.description: Download source packages (for eventual bottling) rather than a bottle

name: --force-bottle
options.description: Download a bottle if it exists for the current or newest version of macOS

name: --retry
options.description: Retry if a download fails or re-download if the checksum of a previously cached version no longer matches

name: --force
-f
options.description: Remove a previously cached version and re-fetch

name: --no-quarantine
options.description: Disable quarantining of downloads (cask)

subcommands.name: reinstall
subcommands.description: Uninstall and then reinstall a formula or cask using the same options it was originally installed with

name: --cask
options.description: Treat all named arguments as casks

name: --formula
options.description: Treat all named arguments as formulae

name: --build-from-source
-s
options.description: Compile formula from source even if a bottle is available

name: --force-bottle
options.description: Install from a bottle if it exists

name: --keep-tmp
options.description: Retain the temporary files created during installation

name: --force
-f
options.description: Install without checking for previously installed keg-only or non-migrated versions

name: --overwrite
options.description: Delete files that already exist in the prefix while linking

name: --no-quarantine
options.description: Disable quarantining of downloads (cask)

name: --skip-cask-deps
options.description: Skip installing cask dependencies

name: --verbose
-v
options.description: Print the verification and post-install steps

name: --version
options.description: Print the version number of Homebrew

name: --verbose
-v
options.description: Make some output more verbose

name: --debug
-d
options.description: Display any debugging information

name: --quiet
-q
options.description: Make some output more quiet

name: --help
-h
options.description: Show help message

name: --env
options.description: Summarise Homebrew's build environment as a plain list

name: --prefix
options.description: Print the install prefix for Homebrew

name: --cellar
options.description: Print the location of Homebrew's Cellar

name: --caskroom
options.description: Print the location of Homebrew's Caskroom

name: --repository
--repo
options.description: Print the location of the active Homebrew repository

name: --cache
options.description: Print the location of Homebrew's download cache
